name: schema-research
description: Schema.org research assistant for Logseq Template Graph. Investigates Schema.org classes and properties, suggests standard vocabulary, validates hierarchies, and provides integration guidance. Use when adding new classes/properties, researching Schema.org standards, or planning template expansions.
capabilities: Fetches Schema.org class definitions and properties
Maps Schema.org types to Logseq property types
Suggests module placement for new classes
author: C0ntr0lledCha0s
securitySummary: This skill provides documentation and guidance for researching Schema.org vocabulary. All static findings are false positives: (1) Backticks in markdown files are code formatting, not Ruby shell execution operators; (2) References to weak crypto in documentation are educational mentions, not actual implementation; (3) WebFetch calls to schema.org are intentional research behavior; (4) Grep searches for template files are legitimate development workflow. The two helper scripts only open URLs in browser. No malicious code detected.
valueStatement: Building Logseq templates requires understanding Schema.org standards. This skill researches classes, properties, and hierarchies to ensure proper integration. It fetches official definitions and provides actionable integration guidance.
limitations: Requires manual WebFetch calls to schema.org

useCases.title: Research new Schema.org classes
useCases.description: Investigate Recipe, MedicalCondition, or any Schema.org class before adding to templates

useCases.title: Find missing properties for classes
useCases.description: Discover what Schema.org properties are missing from Person, Organization, or Event classes

useCases.title: Validate type mappings
useCases.description: Ensure proper Logseq type mapping and inheritance for Schema.org vocabulary

promptTemplates.title: Research new class
promptTemplates.prompt: Research the Recipe class from Schema.org. Show its hierarchy, properties, and recommend how to integrate it into our Logseq template.

promptTemplates.title: Find missing properties
promptTemplates.prompt: What Schema.org properties are missing from our Person class? Compare with the official Schema.org definition and prioritize the most important ones.

promptTemplates.title: Validate type mapping
promptTemplates.prompt: Should birthDate be :date or :default type in Logseq? Check Schema.org and explain the best mapping.

promptTemplates.title: Module placement
promptTemplates.prompt: Where should I add the MedicalCondition class? Analyze the hierarchy and recommend the best module placement.

bestPractices: Always fetch the latest Schema.org definition before adding classes
Check existing template structure to avoid duplicates
Map Schema.org types correctly to Logseq types (:default, :node, :date, :url, :number)
antiPatterns: Don't add classes without researching Schema.org hierarchy first
Don't create properties without checking if they already exist
Don't ignore cardinality guidelines from Schema.org examples

faq.question: Is this skill compatible with all Logseq versions?
faq.answer: Yes, it provides research guidance that works with any Logseq DB version supporting templates.

faq.question: What are the limitations of Schema.org research?
faq.answer: The skill researches vocabulary but cannot automatically create or modify template files.

faq.question: Can I integrate this with my existing templates?
faq.answer: Yes, use the research output to manually add classes and properties to your templates.

faq.question: Is my data safe when using this skill?
faq.answer: Yes, the skill only researches public Schema.org vocabulary and does not access your private data.

faq.question: What if Schema.org vocabulary changes?
faq.answer: The skill fetches live data from Schema.org, ensuring you get the most current definitions.

faq.question: How does this compare to manual Schema.org research?
faq.answer: It automates the research process and provides Logseq-specific integration guidance.
